ddls:
CREATE TABLE "incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "incident_type" TEXT /* Type of incident (e.g., officer-involved shooting, use of force) */,
  "oir_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the incident */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the incident occurred */,
  "reported_by" TEXT /* Person who reported the incident */,
  "allegations" TEXT /* Allegations or synopsis of the incident */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the investigation */,
  "oir_recommendation" TEXT /* OIR recommendation for the incident */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the investigation */,
  "discipline" TEXT /* Discipline taken against the officer */,
  "subsequent_history" TEXT /* Subsequent history related to the incident */,
  "related_civil_criminal" TEXT /* Related civil or criminal investigations */,
  "evidence_attached" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if evidence is attached */,
  "department_involved" TEXT /* Department involved in the incident */,
  "officer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the involved officer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_officer_id FOREIGN KEY ("officer_id") REFERENCES officers ("officer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "investigators" (
  "investigator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each investigator */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident the investigator is assigned to */,
  "investigator_name" TEXT /* Name of the investigator */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the investigator (e.g., lead investigator, supporting investigator) */,
  "assignment_date" TEXT /* Date the investigator was assigned to the incident */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information of the investigator */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the investigator belongs to */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the investigator's involvement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("investigator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_investigators_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "officers" (
  "officer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each officer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the officer */,
  "badge_number" TEXT /* Badge number of the officer */,
  "rank" TEXT /* Rank of the officer */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the officer belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("officer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "evidence" (
  "evidence_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each piece of evidence */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident the evidence is related to */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the evidence */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path of the evidence */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the evidence was uploaded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("evidence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_evidence_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., investigator, supervisor, administrator) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Type of incident (e.g., officer-involved shooting, use of force)
Allegations or synopsis of the incident
Subsequent history related to the incident
Flag indicating if evidence is attached
ID of the incident the investigator is assigned to
Role of the investigator (e.g., lead investigator, supporting investigator)
Date the investigator was assigned to the incident
Current status of the investigator's involvement
Unique identifier for each piece of evidence
ID of the incident the evidence is related to
Role of the user (e.g., investigator, supervisor, administrator)
